assessmentTypes: External infrastructure
Public IPs, domains, VPN, mail, DNS, internet-facing cloud endpoints.
External attack surface and compliance testing.
Internal infrastructure
Internal ranges, AD, servers, endpoints, network devices, segmentation.
Assumed breach, insider risk, PCI segmentation, identity attack paths.
Web application
Web portals, admin consoles, workflows, roles, files, payments, reports.
OWASP WSTG / Top 10 plus business logic.
API / web services
REST, GraphQL, SOAP, gRPC, webhooks, integrations, auth scopes.
OWASP API Top 10, BOLA, tenant isolation, abuse cases.
Mobile application
iOS, Android, app build, backend APIs, local storage, platform protections.
OWASP MASVS/MASTG coverage.
Cloud / Kubernetes
AWS, Azure, GCP, SaaS, Kubernetes, IAM, storage, logging, network.
CIS benchmarks, cloud attack paths, control plane review.
SIEM validation
Rules, log sources, alerts, ATT&CK techniques, SOC workflow, response.
Purple team and detection assurance.
Attack emulation
Threat actor, objectives, TTPs, white cell, safety limits, success criteria.
Threat-led red team or TLPT-style testing.
Specialist assessment
Wireless, physical, social, OT/ICS, code review, tabletop, custom.
Use when standard technical sections do not fit.

domains.title: External Infrastructure
domains.trigger: Use for public IPs, domains, remote access, mail, DNS, cloud public endpoints, or external compliance testing.
capture: Targets
IP ranges, domains, subdomains, cloud endpoints, externally exposed services.
Context
Business owner, hosting provider, WAF/CDN, internet exposure, criticality.
Depth
Discovery only, vulnerability validation, exploit validation, compliance evidence.
Limits
Rate limits, lockout risk, provider terms, third-party approvals, DoS exclusions.
Evidence
Service inventory, screenshots, request/response, PoC output, affected asset IDs.

domains.title: Internal Infrastructure and Identity
domains.trigger: Use for internal network, assumed breach, Active Directory, segmentation, VPN, Wi-Fi, firewall, or endpoint testing.
capture: Targets
CIDRs, VLANs, sites, AD domains, forests, trusts, privileged groups, key servers.
Access
On-site, VPN, jump host, assumed-breach device, standard user, admin account.
Critical zones
CDE, ePHI, CUI, finance, production, OT/ICS, backups, identity systems.
Allowed actions
Scanning, credential capture, lateral movement, privilege escalation, segmentation paths.
Evidence
Attack path, segmentation pass/fail, affected hosts, logs, screenshots, command output.

domains.title: Web Applications
domains.trigger: Use for web portals, customer applications, admin interfaces, authenticated workflows, and business logic.
capture: Targets
Application names, URLs, environments, owners, release window, source/code access.
Roles
User roles, admin roles, tenants, organizations, authorization boundaries.
Workflows
Login, MFA, password reset, file upload, payment, approval, export, reporting.
Data
PII, PHI, cardholder data, financial data, credentials, documents, logs.
Evidence
OWASP coverage, screenshots, HTTP requests/responses, reproduction steps.

domains.title: APIs and Web Services
domains.trigger: Use for REST, GraphQL, SOAP, gRPC, webhooks, batch interfaces, and partner integrations.
capture: Targets
Base URLs, endpoint list, methods, schemas, specs, collections, versions.
Auth
OAuth/OIDC, JWT, API keys, mTLS, scopes, claims, service accounts.
Authorization
Object ownership, tenant isolation, role boundaries, function-level access.
Safety
State-changing calls, destructive methods, rate limits, test data, partner callbacks.
Evidence
Endpoint coverage, PoC requests/responses, schema gaps, affected objects.

domains.title: Mobile Applications
domains.trigger: Use for iOS, Android, mobile APIs, MDM-controlled apps, or device-bound workflows.
capture: Targets
App name, platform, build, bundle/package ID, device requirements, backend APIs.
Access
App binaries, test accounts, device enrollment, MDM, MFA, proxy permissions.
Coverage
Local storage, crypto, network security, platform interaction, auth/session, resilience.
Restrictions
Root/jailbreak limits, production data, app store terms, third-party SDKs.
Evidence
Screens, local storage proof, traffic captures, MASVS coverage, backend findings.

domains.title: Cloud and Kubernetes
domains.trigger: Use for AWS, Azure, GCP, SaaS control planes, Kubernetes, serverless, storage, IAM, or cloud networking.
capture: Targets
Accounts, subscriptions, projects, regions, clusters, services, environments.
Access
Read-only role, security audit role, break-glass policy, MFA, time-limited access.
Focus
IAM, public exposure, storage, secrets, network, logging, CI/CD, containers.
Limits
No production changes, no resource deletion, no live exploit unless approved.
Evidence
Control gaps, IAM paths, screenshots, config exports, log settings, asset IDs.
